message:
fix(connector,runtime): harden numeric safety and prevent unbounded growth across subsystems

Systematic hardening pass addressing overflow, NaN/Inf propagation, and
unbounded collection growth discovered during audit:

BOCPD detector (bocpd.rs):
- Add is_finite() guards to hazard_lambda, kappa_n, alpha_n, sigma_sq,
  and categorical total to prevent NaN/Inf poisoning the posterior
- Auto-reinitialize collapsed posterior (all-zero/NaN) to [1.0, 0, ...]
  so the detector recovers instead of dying permanently
- Guard current_regime_sum and old_mean against non-finite drift

Perf budget guard (perf_budget_guard.rs):
- Return 0.0 overhead when baseline p95/p99 denominators are non-finite,
  preventing NaN propagation into budget decisions

Canonical serializer (canonical_serializer.rs):
- Replace bytes.len() != 4 + len with saturating_sub(4) to prevent
  underflow panic on buffers shorter than 4 bytes

Durability controller (durability.rs):
- Replace min_acks + 2 with saturating_add(2) in quorum matrix (4 sites)

N-version oracle (n_version_oracle.rs):
- Cap divergences at 4096 and block_reasons at 256 via push_bounded()
- Add length prefix to digest_bytes() for domain separation
- Use saturating_add for all severity and receipt counters

Obligation tracker (obligation_tracker.rs):
- Reject duplicate obligation IDs after next_id wraps via saturating_add

Activation pipeline (activation_pipeline.rs):
- Add push_bounded() helper; cap mounted secrets via MAX_MOUNTED_SECRETS

Anti-entropy reconciler (anti_entropy.rs):
- Replace += 1 with saturating_add(1) for rejected/applied counters

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
